reviews.review_title: Very fun but it crashes/screen freezes
reviews.review_description: Love the game! Sadly after level 1 every time I get good upgrades the game crashes or  the screen freezes which just make it bothersome to keep playing:(

This game is a lot of fun. Quite hard, it takes multiple tries to beat each level. Multiplayer is also fun and helps clear levels faster. The main issue is that the game is subject to frequent crashes or major glitches which require closing and restarting the game, and thus having to replay the level over again from the beginning. Multiplayer is even more unstable.
Love playing this with my daughter, so much fun killing the different piñatas, unlocking new weapons. The graphics are great and the levels are well designed. One of my favorite games!

This is a vampire survivors like. It's simpleenough fortune kids, but has enough. Depth for an adult. The "but" comes from the seven assuming we are all kids, I think, because there is no way to turn the day one hint giving announcer off. By the third run, I don't need to be reminded to look behind me. Devs, please put the voice audio on a seporate adjustable/mutable track, so I can play with the rest of the fun audio the game has to offer without the voice audio repetition driving my ADD insane! (No offense to the VA for the announcer, he did a fantastic job. I just don't need him telling me the same hint my 50th time playing. Especially not if I am already playing on hard mode!)

I picked this up on a whim. It looked silly and probably fun. And I was right on both counts. The graphics are nothing to write home about, but you won't notice when you're fighting off hordes of brightly colored donkeys. I've never played Vampire Survivors but I imagine this is what the VR port should have looked like. Also a great workout! (155 calories in 21 minutes of gameplay). Minus one star because for whatever reason trying to hit the "return to garage" button at the end of a level is a mini game in itself. The cursor won't touch it without lots of flailing around, hoping to land on it by accident (it's hard to explain). Please fix this!
The fact the you don't have public play/online play is kinda annoying
Played on a whim and really enjoyed
I love this game i feel powerful when playing this game

reviews.review_title: simple to play, and fun for all.
reviews.review_description: I didn't have high expectations given the rough graphics shown in the description, but it exceeded my expectations and was incredibly fun. The actual visuals are quite adorable; the polygonal design actually gives it a retro and humorous feel. It's satisfying to watch, simple to play, and fun for all.

reviews.review_description: I have played some rougelite games but this is for children and is soo fun instead of things Luke blade and sorcery vr gore and for parents "never rate the game by its reviews becuase people play games and think its soo good but never rates it thats why I or we with there was how many visits or active players there is on meta ok? Thank you super happy, buy this game immediately 😀 😄  byeee.

I really enjoyed this game! There are lots of different weapons and upgrades to get and try, the characters are super fun, and I got hooked into doing "just one more run" wanting to do better and unlock more stuff. I highly recommend! Extra bonus points for working well in a small space.

reviews.review_title: Love it love the game but just a bit
reviews.review_description: aight so its a bit buggy bc there are invis enemys

honestly wish they would add more content i'm almost done beating all three difficulties on each level wilson the weapons have unique classes and your play style will match a certain one. but i definitely hope they add more content in the future. if the dev can answer me on below one simple question is there gonna be a part two or will there be more content.
funny
This game is amazing is it worth almost $40? YES, the game has an amazing theme being candy and it’s multiplayer which’s makes it so much funnier

Honestly enjoying playing this. Love the arcade style and feel, like how you unlock stuff etc it's just fun to play.
However getting the achievements is very very difficult!

this game is so much fun you can fight pinatas and unlock maps
you should guys make a second one

reviews.review_description: The gameplay is quite fun and chaotic and I enjoyed it heavily the weopens worked well and there's lots of builds people can set up.
the game is too long you should put less time like around 10 minutes
game is fine but it doesn't let you join or play with random player. You have to try to get a friend to get the game and have them host the room for you to join in.

reviews.review_description: Best game ever

reviews.review_description: Shoot little pinatas
This game is awesome its just, there's nobody in multiplayer mode though 😐😔🥴
This game is epic!!! Absolutely love it 20 minute test turned into 2 hours, it's fun, funny and great to play 100/10 great job 👍😄👍
Fun game

developer_response.body: Thanks for the 5 stars, blakeyblake7! 🎉 We’re thrilled you had a nice, fun time smashing pinatas in VR — keep swinging! 🪅🕶️

reviews.review_description: Slay

developer_response.body: Thanks for the 5 stars, TZYbob!🔥 We’re glad you’re loving Rogue Piñatas!

reviews.review_description: Absolutely vrmazing experience

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the 5 stars and the awesome feedback, BooBear116! 🌟 We’re thrilled you had a “vrmazing” time smashing piñatas with us!

reviews.review_description:
Been meaning to leave a review for a while. This game was not on my radar before release… the premise seemed a little silly.  I took a chance and boy I’m happy I did. Easily one of the most addictive games on Quest.  The constant upgrades during rounds are like little hits of sum-sumthin ;)  There’s a few weapons that are super clever as well, my favorite being the Jukelele…. A guitar you swing that inflicts damage to everything in a 10m radius. So fun lol.  The game offers 3 difficulty settings for each level, all with their own objectives. Each difficulty level has 3 objectives, making for 9 total objectives per level.  The weapon combinations is where this game really shines though. Once you upgrade a certain weapon to its max, you’ll get a super upgrade, making your weapon the ultimate destructive force. Im 40 (41 actually… shut up lol) and I’ve already logged about 12 hrs of game time. This easily offers at least 15-20 hrs to 100% the game.   

Really hope this game receives a bunch of future updates and dlc.  It’s one of those games I’d gladly purchase dlc that offered new weapons and levels.  Great job devs!

As a side note, I wanted to mention how cool it is to be able to discuss the game with the actual dev on their discord in real time.  Every question and criticism I had, they responded within 30 mins.  The friendliest developer ever. These are the type of games I LOVE supporting, and VR needs more devs like this.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this awesome review,PowPow55! 🎉 We’re beyond thrilled that Rogue Piñatas found its way into your headset (and your heart). The Jukelele is a fan favorite for a reason - shredding enemies with sound never gets old!

We’re cooking up more chaos, weapons, and surprises, so stay tuned. And seriously, your support and energy fuel our dev hammers - thanks for being part of this sweet, swinging community! ❤️

reviews.review_description: One of the better game to play with friends. Good amount of characters and weapons to unlock, with the shield u have to use melee which for me is real nice as it makes u not too dependent of certain weapons. Different types of enemies were really nice, always excited when seeing a new one. Physics feels nice and music isn't boring, a must try for me.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the AWESOME review, The_Only_Wei! We’re glad you appreciate our weapon design and enemy variety - we had just as much fun designing them! ✅ 🧁

reviews.review_description:
Rogue piñatas is simply the VR game that played that scratched that Vampire Survivor itch .

A relatively simple game that just involves you killing as many enemies as you can to level up. 
Each level up rewards you with more weapons and perks to help you fend off the hoards of rogue piñatas.

Weapons are split into melee and ranged types and can level up multiple times, to increase their effectiveness.

In Vampire survivor tradition, every weapon has a counterpart perk.
By maxing the weapon level and having the associated perk you can evolve that said weapon to something really special.

You gain currency on each run to level up your character permanently.
Otherwise in Bullet heaven tradition, you start every level from level zero and have to level up from scatch every run.

With 5 characters to unlock and 6 stages to play through and multiple difficulty settings......theres plenty to grind through here.

You can also play the game multiplayer with your friends.

I wont lie.....the game has some aggressive adaptive resolution and dips in the frame rate but the core game is still very fun.
Its also a blast in multiplayer.
A solid game for me but if the dev can just iron out some of the performance issues (mainly in 4 player co op.....2/3 is better) it would be a 5 star game for me.

Heres hoping that the game gets these improvements and some surprise content updates drops in the near future!(in Vampire Survivor tradition!l)

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the FANTASTIC review, Burntbreadman! 🎉 We're thrilled that Rogue Piñatas hit that Vampire Survivors sweet spot for you. 

We hear you on the performance issues, particularly in 4-player co-op, and are actively working on optimizations to make the experience even smoother.

Thanks again for your support and feedback - it means the world to us!

reviews.review_description: I love this game. It's super addictive and I can easily spend hours playing. Mom is my favorite character to play but I love how you can unlock other characters to play with. With so many challenges, I know my daughters and I will have tons of fun! If you're on the fence, take the leap!

developer_response.body:
Karma77420, thank you so much for your kind words! We're thrilled to hear that you and your daughters are having a blast! It’s awesome to know Rogue Piñatas is bringing fun and challenges for your family. ❤️

And to anyone on the fence - take Karma's advice and jump in! 😉

reviews.review_description: I wasn't sure what to expect from indie dev Nerd Ninjas, but this game is as addictive as the candy you snag from pinatas. Delightfully humorous, skillfully witty, as dangerously fun. Sorry, I have more to say, but I think I'm gonna dive in and play another round!

developer_response.body:
Wow, what an awesome review - thank you, dsprui11! 🎉 We’re so glad Rogue Piñatas has you hooked (just like the candy inside!) and love that you're enjoying the humor and chaos.

Now, don’t let us keep you - go crush another round! 😆🔥

reviews.review_description:
I have had the ability to test this game. It is really fun. Kill those piñatas and get the candies to upgrade your weapons. 

Big bosses are not that easy, but go in with friends and you can definitely beat them. 
You can select different characters and they have all there own weapon.

An easy game, just run around, kill piñatas and collect candies nearby. 

I recommend this game.

developer_response.body:
What an awesome review, POWERGIRL.BE_HTTA! 🎉🍬 We're so glad you had fun testing the game and taking down those tricky bosses! 💪 With different characters, weapons, and plenty of piñatas to smash, there's always more fun to be had.

Thanks for the recommendation! 🔥🥳

reviews.review_description:
This is such a fun game when played both solo or in multiplayer. A typical rouge-like, you are dropped into each mission area with one weapon and a goal. You defeat pinatas, collect their candy and level up, then are presented with a choice of 3 upgrades. These can be stacked to create some truly powerful weapons. My favourite so far has been the 'vampire bat', a melee weapon that heals you on each contact hit with an enemy...I became unstoppable!

The fact that the choices are random means that there's lots of replayability of the missions, going back and trying out different weapons combinations.

I love the cartoony styling and character designs, the pinatas are really funny and the boss designs are great. 

I have experienced some frame rate drops when there have been huge hordes of enemies on screen but I think this is something the devs will be able to iron out in future updates. I'm looking forward to seeing what levels are released in the future.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the FANTASTIC review, motion_Megan! 🎉 It's awesome to hear that you're having a blast both solo and in multiplayer. And the Vampire Bat is definitely a game changer - unstoppable, indeed! 🦇💥 Thanks again for your support!

reviews.review_title: The game was very fun but...
reviews.review_description: I found the game very fun and all but the graphics seem to hurt my head focusing on an enemy piñata or the whole map overall. melee weapons seem to get stuck at walls and trees at time if you ever hit those corners, but sticking to the positive side this game is very fun and I cannot wait to share this game with my family and expect more to come for this title!

developer_response.body:
Yoshi1673, thanks for the honest feedback and the kind words! 🎯 

We’re pumped you had fun and can’t wait to hear how your family gets into the chaos too. We hear you on the visuals and melee quirks - definitely things we're looking at as we keep polishing the piñata-popping madness. Appreciate you sticking with us! 💥

reviews.review_description: Rogue Piñatas is a funny action packed game that has the player survive waves of rogue piñatas that get harder to beat over time. It's got a good amount of weapon selection, both melee and ranged with each one having its own unique way of dealing damage. The game can be a bit confusing at the very beginning with it's mechanics, specifically the evolution of weapons but you can quickly learn them in the heat of a battle. There was an issue with multiplayer in the last level where the objective was stuck on my friends screen at a random place that it had moved away from a long time ago. I experienced another issue and it's that my quest 2 battery ran out pretty quick compared to other games even though it was at 100% when I started. It can be just that my VR headset is a bit old but still it gave me enough time to really enjoy the game and it left me whishing my headset could charge faster so that I can hop back in to slay some more rogue piñatas. There are some bugs that are pretty typical in VR games that don't really impact the gameplay much but can be noticed easily. Overall a great game to spend some time with friends and it can be a game that is repayable with how much it has.

developer_response.body: Thanks for diving into the piñata pandemonium, lol3980! We’re patching up bugs and tweaking battery gobblers, but am glad you still had a smashing time! 🔋🍬🎮

reviews.review_description:
So, I did not expect this game to have a roguelike upgrade system like from Vampire Survivors. Generally game is in a GREAT state, as of now, and it is most definitely worth a try.
All of the weapons in there are very fun to use, each in their own unique way. I love the physics and the knockback on the enemies!
The game (for me) is playable for about 3 whole matches, after which either my battery runs out or it just gets too repetitive.
I've noticed that during boss fights (sometimes) the audio is coming from the opposite side to where the boss is relative to the player, when I played it the game also had clipping issues where the player would get stuck on certain objects found across the map.
I played this game in multiplayer mode which enabled me to try the revive, it works fine but it seems that if you don't get to the revive spot in time (which provides no real indicator besides the barely visible countdown on the top of the screen) the game just automatically ends with no REAL visual or sound cues. This SUCKS. Talking about game end screens, it shows some useful info but there is no way for you to tell that you DIED, no animation, no nothing, just a GUI popping up from nowhere ending the game.
To this day it is unclear for me how to evolve/mutate weapons, this info was spewed out by the AI character and never specified. The blue and purple cubes around the map make no sense to me too, I have no idea what they do/what is their exact purpose.
Without looking at the wonky stuff, Id say that the game really needs a proper tutorial and guides. I liked the GUI for the level selection, it was classy in a good way and had an intriguing way of selecting harder difficulties.
Another mention, I have no idea if that's a bug, but the missions which firstly were shown on the second stage, switched around and now are on the first stage. This may be something unclear SPECIFICALLY to me, but that stunned me a bit.
Do try this game, its fun and definitely worth it.

developer_response.body:
Taigerscat, thank you for the detailed feedback! It helps us make things even better! 
We’re squashing bugs and working on a more helpful tutorial. 🔧🐛💥

Keep whacking!

reviews.review_description: have to use one hand for the weapon    as far as i can tell you cant change the hand the weapon is in while you play

developer_response.body:
Thanks a ton for playing and sharing your thoughts, Stormcat519! 🙌 

You can actually swap between the weapons you’ve picked up during a run by pressing 'A' on the right controller or 'X' on the left. Definitely give it a try next time you jump in! 🎮🎉

reviews.review_title: Just what I've been waiting for

developer_response.body: Appreciate you jumping into the piñata frenzy, CRACK_BABY! Happy to hear you’re getting both cardio and chaos - gotta love a candy-powered workout! 🍫💪

reviews.review_description: Played for around 4 hours and still have so much more to learn about weapons and characters in game. Absolute blast destroying piñatas and discovering cool weapon upgrades. Highly recommend the game and look forward to playing many more hours!

developer_response.body: We appreciate your recommendation, DenimDanger0501! 🎉 So thrilled you're having a blast smashing piñatas and experimenting with all the weapons and characters. There’s definitely plenty more to discover, and we can’t wait for you to dive even deeper! 🔥🍬

reviews.review_description: I really had a blast in this game, I can't wait to play it with my boys in multiplayer.  I even enjoy trying to last as long as I can on my own.  Can't wait to see more of what you guys come up with.  More levels and more bosses please. This is a favorite for sure.

developer_response.body: Glad you’re loving the solo and squad smashes, Slim1337! More bosses and levels are on the way - let’s keep the fiesta rolling! 🎊🍬

reviews.review_description:
Played it solo and with a friend, both was fun, but it is definitely more fun with a friend. Depending on the amount of candy you collect you get upgrades in the form of different cards to choose from, just like you know from other rogue-likes. You get different ranged and melee weapons.

To unlock new characters in the base, you have to kill a ton of enemies with a certain kind of weapon; check the character infos to find out what weapon you need to choose in a run. The weapons needed are also the default weapons of those characters.

Performance was good, the different upgrades can stack and become better over time. If you know other rogue-likes, you know what you can expect in terms of upgrades. Had a lot of fun already and will definitely play much more of this.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review, Tryyton! We're glad you're having fun both solo and with a friend - co-op definitely adds to the experience! 🎉 It’s great to hear you’re enjoying the upgrade system, weapon variety, and character unlocks and we hope you continue having a blast smashing piñatas! 🍬🔥

reviews.review_description:
Rogue Piñatas is the delightful, chaotic party apocalypse you didn't know you needed! Every match is a whirlwind of candy-colored craziness, where teamwork, quick reflexes, and strategic use of gadgets and weapons are your best friends. The unique piñatas are charmingly ferocious, each with unpredictable attacks that keep the action fresh and exciting. Taking down massive Mega Piñatas, especially the intimidating Bull, is both hilarious and thrilling. Whether you're battling solo or teaming up with friends, Rogue Piñatas offers endless replay ability and plenty of laughs. 

When I wasn't laughing, I was running for my life. Or maybe both... lol. It's a must play and bring some friends with you. If no one will go in with you, join their discord and find someone. 

Get ready for a sugar rush of fun!

developer_response.body: This review made us laugh and cheer - thank you SO much, TheGamingVeteran! May your sugar rushes be wild and your Mega Piñatas be many! 🐂🍬

reviews.review_description:
It's a zombie (or vampire) apacolypse, but with piñatas! I'm really happy about that because I'm pretty sick of Zombies. The graphics and character designs are great and they look like an intentional choice, not a compromise.

It starts off simple but things quickly go crazy. You are constantly leveling up and you have a random selection of powerups each level. There is some strategy to this because there are party box defense areas that also give you enhancements, but they are more focused to what upgrades you already have. It's good to level up to get a direction started, then use the party boxes to upgrade your chosen path.

There's good variety in the missions, at least so far. The first mission is timed, the second one has you repairing generators, and the third has you defending a vehicle. I have only just barely made it to the third mission, but the level and pinata attackers change with each level. There are also secrets to find in the levels.

Just last night I finally got to play multiplayer with my son and that was a blast. It's basically the same game, but now there are two of you. I'm not sure how well it scales for two players, since it seemed much easier, but we had fun goofing around. 

There are two reasons it didn't get that 5th star. It's a bit glitchy sometimes. Most of the time it's kind of fun as piñatas get stuck easily, but the second level bosses just kept running into a building and never actually attacked me. They worked fine when I played them with my son, though. Also, I think they could do just a little more to explain things in the game. There are some monster drops that I still don't know what they do. There is a narrator that comments during the game and helps to explain things, but it needs just a little more. A dedicated tutorial or even just a help page would fix this.

developer_response.body:
Wow, tpancoast! Thank you for the thoughtful and detailed review! 👏 We're so glad you're enjoying the piñata twist on the classic apocalypse and that the variety in missions and character designs is resonating with you. It’s also awesome to hear that you had fun playing multiplayer with your son! 🎮🎮

We appreciate your feedback on the glitches and the need for more in-game explanations. Our team is already working on addressing those issues, and your suggestion for a dedicated tutorial or help page is great - gonna definitely keep that in mind. 📝 From Nerd Ninjas, thanks again for your support and for taking the time to share your experience!

reviews.review_description:
Really happy to have been one of the playtesters for this game.  This is the first game I've played that tries being like Vampire Survivors (a "Survivors-like") but in VR, and I think they've done an excellent job.  

You're often surrounded but your weapons get progressively more powerful as you play.  You also get the usual roguelite upgrades where you can get permanent boosts using currency you earn playing the game--making the next run a bit easier.  There's a great variety of weapons to fit different playstyles and there are different levels that have varying primary objectives like escorting a vehicle or protecting locations.

Highly recommend this game.  It's a lot of fun.

developer_response.body: AroraMew, thank you so much for your kind words and for being part of the playtesting! 🎉 We're thrilled that you enjoyed the Survivors-like experience in VR and had fun experimenting with different weapons and objectives. Your continued support and recommendation mean a lot to us! 😊

reviews.review_description: The game starts very well by showing you the basics of how you are supposed to attack the pinatas and gives a slight backstory. You are then thrown into a garage where you select upgrades characters and multiplayer or single player. The game menus are very nice and easy to use, then when you are actually in the game everything feels smooth, the swinging has no flaws and ends up where you would expect and the ranged weapons are pretty good. The different waves of pinatas are very unique and the different abilities and characters are nice to have. The progression system can keep you coming to the game multiple times with the different upgrades and characters you are likely never to run out of content if you enjoy wave based survival games. I'd recommend this to anyone looking for a wave based survival game other than just the normal zombie survival.

developer_response.body:
Snickersallday, thank you so much for this detailed and thoughtful review! 💖 

We’re thrilled you’re enjoying the smooth swings, sweet progression, and piñata-packed chaos—way more colorful than zombies, right? 😉🍬

reviews.review_description:
This game brilliantly captures the addictive 'bullet heaven' gameplay loop popularized by Vampire Survivors, but gives it a fun, festive twist – you're battling waves of colorful pinatas instead of monsters! 

The core gameplay is incredibly engaging, and the progression system feels rewarding, constantly dangling new unlocks and fun upgrades that keep each run feeling fresh and exciting. I initially found the teleport movement system a bit tricky to manage in the heat of the action, but switching to smooth locomotion made a world of difference and significantly improved my enjoyment.

If you're looking for a great VR experience to satisfy that VS craving, definitely give this one a try!

developer_response.body: NuggzFPV, thank you for the kind words and great feedback! We’re so glad the sweet chaos clicked - smooth locomotion for the win! 🚶‍♂️💨

reviews.review_description: Rouge Piñatas is amazing, fun, and funny to play. There are many things to do in this game with different levels.   Multiplayer mode is very fun to play with your friends. I never knew destroying piñatas could be so amazing. This game is very addictive to play.

developer_response.body: Hey DuccThe1st, thank you for all your wonderful comments! 👍 We had just as much fun creating the piñatas as you've had destroying them! 🔥🍬 So glad you and your friends enjoy the mayhem and all that our game has to offer.

reviews.review_description: This game is great its so much fun to me its a workout and a game all in one  so thats even better . I like that you can pick your character and weapon .I like that you can play single player,  multiplayer or co op the choice is yours.  The graphics are superb.  If you're like me you will forget about the time because this game is so much fun you will want to keep going to see what comes next when you level up .

developer_response.body: Thank you for the amazing review, skyvuc! 🌟 We're so glad you're having fun and getting a great workout in the process! 💪🍬 It’s awesome to hear you enjoy the variety of characters, weapons, and play modes. Our team appreciates your kind words about the graphics and are thrilled the game keeps you hooked! 🎣

reviews.review_description: I like the game, very fast and fun gameplay, I use this with my roto vr explorer chair and it is a blast.  I am looking forward to playing more multiplayer with friends and looking forward to updates and new levels, the dev team has put in a lot of effort on this game and it shows.

developer_response.body: Whitebeardvr, thanks for the sweet support! 🍭 Glad you're enjoying the candy carnage with your VR setup - we’ve got more madness on the way! 💥🚀

reviews.review_description: It was great watching over time new updates coming in and making the game better and loved the characters and their ultimates, but I wish for new characters and an easier way to unlock their ultimate, and to tell to you what you the requirements is for the ultimates for their weapon.

developer_response.body: Windkif, thank you for your awesome review and for following along with the updates! 🎉 We appreciate your feedback on unlocking characters and the desire for clearer requirements - our game design team definitely keep that in mind for future updates. Thanks again for your support! 😊

reviews.review_description:
A really fun grindy game. 
There are characters and upgrades to unlock by completing challenges.
You have to grind points to buy upgrades.
When you're strong enough to beat a level you unlock another one that is a bit more challenging.

I wish the environment was a bit more destructible though. Sometimes the only thing between me and a POI is fence and I get lost in map looking for a way around that fence. 


Every level I played so far was unique in design and object which was awesome. 
. 

Love how active the development is with the frequent updates. Please add more levels!

developer_response.body:
Majed6, thanks a ton for the thoughtful feedback! 🎉 We’re glad you’re enjoying the grind, the unlocks, and the unique level designs! 

Noted on destructible environments and tricky fences - we’re always looking to smash through barriers (literally)! 💥🍭

reviews.review_description: This game has a fun loop, with a good variety of weapons, enemies, and levels. Also, Depending on your chosen weapon it can be a pretty decent cardio workout with melee weapons getting quite physical. Cool color pallete and soundtrack. Ran into a couple bugs but nothing major. If you like plenty of action and bashing things, this game is for you.

developer_response.body: Flippynuggets, thank you for the awesome review! 🎉 We’re so glad you're enjoying the fun loop, variety, and physicality of the melee weapons. And it's great to hear that the color palette and soundtrack are hitting the right notes too! 🎨🎵 Our team appreciates you mentioning the bugs and will keep working on ironing those out. 💪

reviews.review_description:
The premise is obviously very simple, just kill everything in sight, but sometimes the simple games are some of the best ones and Rogue Piñatas is a lot of fun.
As you'd probably expect, there is a full range of bright colours in this game and combined with the cartoony art style, they compliment each other nicely and really pop out in the world.
You have numerous weapons to unlock and characters to play, half a dozen maps to play through along with each area having a boss at the end of each zone.
I would say it's a very family friendly game, parents could easily play with their kid and all have a blast saving the neighbourhood from an army of rabid Piñatas, it does have some issues, framerate stuff going on, but I definitely think they can sort those out and make it even better than it already is.

developer_response.body: Love the headline! 🎉 Thank you for the wonderful review, AllUAreIsHistory! We're so glad you're enjoying the colorful, cartoony chaos of Rogue Piñatas and that it's a fun experience for the whole family! 🍬💥 We appreciate your feedback on the framerate, and we're working on optimizations to make the game even better.

reviews.review_description: This game is simple in execution, kill enemies, progress or die, and level up.  I've played a lot of similar game types and this definitely scratches the rogue lite itch.  There are plenty of areas to progress and upgrade, tons of fun weapons and a lot of different weapons to hold interest for many playthroughs.  Co-op for up to 4 players is a blast. there is a wide variety of new characters to unlock as well.  I would recommend to anyone looking for a game in this genre. the characters and level design are fun and colorful.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the ✨ FANTASTIC ✨ review, Sandwichx! We're so glad Rogue Piñatas is scratching that roguelite itch for you.  With tons of weapons, characters, and co-op mayhem, there’s always more fun to be had! Hope you keep enjoying the colorful chaos. 🌈💥

reviews.review_description: Super fun, VR Rogue-lite with the added plus of aiming skills and a ton of weapon and gadget combinations. So fun to blow up scores of pinatas and become god-like rolling thunder against hordes of enemies. A perfect game to pop on when I want to blow off some steam and break stuff, or just go from zero to hero in 10 minutes or so.

This is not your average roguelike. Rogue Piñatas is much more! Apart from the simplistic weapon usage/handling melee works amazingly well. And the gameplay loop proves itself to work smoother than butter., since you keep getting more unique weapons. Despite the focus lying on multiplayer, the stages are incredibly fun on your own, due to how addicting roguelikes are. And then there's the wacky colorful visual style that makes playing like visiting an art exhibit with how amazing the enemies and environment looks.
Now to the downsides: The dynamic resolution you know from flatscreen gaming doesn't work well in VR and breaks the immersion. At least you only have to go through it when the frames drop, which happens, but rarely too.
But all in all, I have found myself enjoying Rogue Piñatas more than I am willing to admit; I have a very critical perspective on games.)

developer_response.body: 🎉 Thank you very much for the fantastic review, Dan.1! 🎉 It's awesome to hear Rogue Piñatas has gone beyond your expectations and that you're enjoying the gameplay loop, visuals, and melee action. 💯🔥 We also appreciate your feedback on dynamic resolution and are working on improvements to make the experience even smoother. Thanks for your support and for being so critical in a positive way - it helps us make the game even better! 💪

reviews.review_description:
I am a big fan of the genre in general, and i am quite satisfied with what i see here. It has a charming theme and look with the piñatas and it feels good just clobbing them one after another.
To the gameplay itself, it takes clear inspiration from vampire survivors in terms of upgrades and progression and especially weapon evolutions. Theres a decent number of weapons which are pretty unique to eqch other which you can even dual wield. different maps not only offer their own kind of piñatas, but also different objectives which offers some variation and not only the classic "survive X minutes".
The problems i have are pretty insignificant. For example on melee it feels too dependent on getting a health regen upgrade or im not a fan of unlocking characters by getting their weapons and doing x kills with them, when your build is set for the character and weapon you already have.
Otherwise, quite fun

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the AWESOME words, gWinnieVR! 💥 

We’re glad that dual-wielding destruction is always a good time and will take your feedback to the drawing board as we continue to improve the game's experience for all players! 🍭👊

reviews.review_description: With a number of characters to choose from, fun and meaningful upgrades and a fair difficulty curve, this game really hits a spot that is missing in the VR gaming catalogue. If you're looking for a game to enjoy in short sessions or you want to challenge yourself with multiple playthroughs, this is a game you will want to check out. It's also appropriate for all ages, so no worrying about any mature themes. All in all, a great game that kids and adults can enjoy.

developer_response.body: Dr_Red_MD, we are very humbled to hear that Rogue Piñatas fill that sweet spot for VR games! 🏆✨ Your recommendation means so much to us and we hope you continue to have a blast, playing all of the different characters and experimenting with weapon upgrades.

reviews.review_title: A bit of fun and exercise
reviews.review_description: I enjoyed this game in single player mode.  Has that cartoony visual style with clean graphics.  It's not deep or technical, just a lot of pinatas, weapon variety, and different characters to unlock to keep the action lively.  Really enjoyed having goals to work towards to unlock the different weapons and characters.  Fun powerups to use on every level.  It was easy to jump into a game and didn't have any issues.  Multiplayer however had some technical bugs trying to get a game going or getting disconnected.  A great dev team so I'm confident that part will get smoothed out.  The different types of weapons and tons of powerups were the highlight for me, I would mix up the weapons I would use to keep the action fresh.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your praise, Potatochip007! 🥔 Our dev team is so proud to have your support and definitely will continue to do our best to make this the best game out there in VR! 🎮 Improving the multiplayer experience is a big focus for us right now and we hope you continue having a blast smashing those piñatas! 🍬💥

reviews.review_description: What a fun game that is so easy to pick up. Within minutes of hopping into the game, I got the mechanics and core gameplay. It was so easy and addictive to hop into a run. The progression is unique and robust and I especially like that there's a multiplayer option!

developer_response.body: Thanks for smashing in with such an awesome review, halabeih! 🎉 We're stoked you picked it up fast and got hooked - just how we like it. Keep whacking those piñatas and climbing the chaos! 💥

reviews.review_title: An Addictive and Thrilling VR Roguelike
reviews.review_description: This is one of the best roguelikes available in VR. With a wide variety of characters and weapons to choose from, you can also unlock max upgrades for your weapons by finding the right combination of upgrades. It's challenging, fun, and incredibly addictive!

developer_response.body: Jezzu, wow, thank you for the stellar review! ✨ We're SO proud to be considered "one of the best roguelikes in VR" and are happy to hear that you're having an incredibly fun experience. Thanks again for all your support! 💯❤️

reviews.review_description:
Well, at least up to a point, as eventually I was able to get some ranged weapons and talk about a difference. Starting out in this game with a bat can certainly wear you out, so clear a little space and practice that swing. But, after a couple level ups, you can easily get one of the many other weapons and not rely on melee, despite the game being about hitting piñatas. I mean, if you had a chance to use a “wand”, a roman candle essentially, to hit a piñata, wouldn’t want to?

This whole game reminds me a lot of the old Xbox/Rare games, Viva Piñata, but they have all gone rogue and now, instead of playing games with them, you have to fight them off. You still get candy out of them and the more candy you get, the stronger you get. 

There are currently 6 levels that can be played normal or extreme, 6 starting characters that all have their unique weapons, a bunch more weapons that you can unlock as you level up and a bunch of different piñata characters to destroy.  With the meta candy you collect throughout the games, you can purchase permanent upgrades that can greatly enhance your odds, but they ramp up in price quick. 

There are a few bugs still in the game, but with the testing I have done with them over the past couple months, it is clear that they are receptive to feedback and looking to make the game better. This one is fun for all ages that can use the headset and can be even better with friends online, provided things don’t bug out there too. Just something that should get better in the coming months.

developer_response.body:
Incredible write-up, Satconcook! Thank you for capturing so much of what the game offers! 

We’re glad you embraced the chaos (and gave your arms a break with some ranged weapons 😅). Yes to the Viva Piñata vibes - we see it too. And yes, we’re constantly updating based on feedback, so thank you for helping shape it!

reviews.review_description: This game is a fun swarm based rogue-lite with bosses and endless waves of enemies makes me feel like I'm playing cod zombies but much more child friendly and in vr. The power ups are very useful and easy to understand what each one does and with 6 playable characters and a lot of weapons there's quite a few ways to play the game, adding in that there's multiplayer this is a great game to play with friends. Fun game for people who want a swarm of pinata’s to beat up

developer_response.body: Thanks a ton for the positive review, lavuy67! 🎉 We love that it gives zombie vibes but with a candy-coated twist—because what’s more fun than smacking piñatas with friends? 🍭💥

reviews.review_title: Pure Piñata Pandemonium – and I’m Addict
reviews.review_description:
Rogue Piñatas is utter chaos in the best way possible. Imagine if Vampire Survivors crashed headfirst into Dead Rising, but with exploding party candy, insane gadgets, and co-op mayhem.

The combat is wildly satisfying, with tons of hilarious and brutal weapons to choose from, and the gadget evolutions? Total game-changers.  The character variety is awesome, the bosses are massive, and there are over 30 different types of piñatas, each with their own deadly tricks (they’re cute until they’re not).   And don't forget the rewards at the end of each round, choose wisely as they are key to making the most of each run.

What really takes it to the next level is the online multiplayer — this game was clearly built for co-op chaos.  Whether you're playing solo or rolling with a squad, it's a blast. 

Rogue Piñatas is hands-down one of the most original and ridiculously fun roguelites in VR.

Buy it. Survive the candy-coated apocalypse. And never trust a piñata again.

developer_response.body:
WOW, what a review—thank you, Todd.Q2CVRGamer! 🤯 We might just print this on a piñata. 

So glad the madness, weapons, and candy apocalypse are keeping you hooked. And yes, we agree with your advice...never trust a piñata....especially the cute lookin' ones... 🎯🍭

reviews.review_description:
Rogue piñatas is a VR game that everybody can play. The game mechanics are straight forward. You clobber pinatas, level up, gain new abilities and weapons. And repeat. There is a good mix of abilities as well as weapon upgrades. Weapons come into two main flavors - melee and ranged. Am glad to see this mix as my play style leans towards ranged weapons. 
There a decent number of characters to unlock, each with their own unique abilities. Coupled with the perks and weapon upgrades that you can tailor to your liking, this gives the game replay value.
There are a decent number of levels though more would also be welcome.
The game is colorful and the character designs are attractive. Technically the game plays well and the controls are responsive. My testing was with the single player mode only so I can't address how the headset holds up under multiplayer runs. 
Overall, a fun game that is easy for anyone to pick and quickly learn the controls. As with all good games, the more time you invest in experimenting with the different characters and loadouts, the more you will get out of the game. The violence is cartoonish and not off-putting in any way. A family friendly game.

developer_response.body: Really appreciate this thoughtful review, OldDarth! 🙌 We're thrilled you enjoyed the variety and family-friendly fun—perfect for anyone ready to clobber with candy-powered style! 🍬💪

reviews.review_title: a favorite to add to your library
reviews.review_description: Rogue Piñatas: VRMageddon delivers a refreshing twist to the multiplayer VR roguelite genre. Set in a chaotic birthday party gone wrong, players must navigate through relentless waves of aggressive piñatas that provide both challenge and humor. I thoroughly enjoyed both single-player and multiplayer modes; the solo experience allowed me to hone my skills, explore the vibrant world, and unlock other characters while teaming up with friends in online multiplayer amplified the fun and strategy as we tackled challenges together. The game shines with its diverse character selection and a robust arsenal of weapons and gadgets, allowing for unique combat strategies. While the mechanics can be frenetic at times, the blend of creativity and chaos offers an engaging survival experience that keeps players coming back for more.

developer_response.body:
WOWOWOW  - this one means a lot! Thanks for such a thoughtful and detailed take, MissChiefVR. ✨

We love that both solo and multiplayer hit home for you, and you really nailed what we were going for with the creative chaos. Hope to see you and your squad back in the candy carnage soon! ❤️💪

reviews.review_description:
Ok seriously, this game is super addicting. First off, it's a roguelite of all things. So you become overpowered within the first level.  Second of all, there's a massive army of enemies that are trying to kill you. Not like Everslaught Invasion or Dead Hook, not even like Hellsweeper (which is my favorite vr game of all time). You are given 20-30+ enemies that are chasing you around and they don't wait. 

The game I would compare this to is definitely Vampire Survivors. This is VR Vampire Survivors except you're killing numerous piñatas Instead of bats. 

Get this, you also have a big variety of weapons you can choose from. 
You start off with the traditional bat (except it's like 6 bats tied to a megabat.
You can also get guns that shoot jawbreakers
You also get a machine gun that's akin to the minigun from Compound (Yeah that super-powered weapon you unlock after earning all the gold achievements)
You also get a wand that shoots homing magic balls on enemies
You also get a punching glove that sends shockwaves in the direction of your punches
AND THE LIST GOES ON.

The bosses are also super huge and they function similar to what you would see in a FFXIV boss. Aka do massive damage against them constantly because they're that buffed up.

If you want a game where you don't need to use your brain much, if you want to let out your chimp-neurons... BUY THIS GAME

Wow! This game is so unique and just the right amount of challenging.  If these piñatas were zombies, they could be terrifying. I love that it has goals & objectives. (Be sure to read your objectives before you start.) Leveling up is very satisfying. The weapons are unique; I can’t wait to play more of the characters. I’ve enjoyed this in single and multiplayer modes. FYI - I play this sitting down and have to be careful to not have things within arms reach that I might knock over. The piñatas come from all different directions. Watch out for the mushrooms because they are the real killers.  ;)
Highly recommend.  I’ve never played a game quite like this.

developer_response.body:
Haha, the mushrooms have claimed many unsuspecting players! 😈 Glad you picked up on the variety of objectives and enemy behaviors - we wanted to make every run feel a little different.

Thanks for the kind words and the mushroom PSA,Veronica.25!

reviews.review_description:
This is exactly what I needed without evening knowing it.  This game is easy to spend hours upon hours grinding out the characters and upgrades.  While doing so it keeps you entertained.  The difficulty rises are decent with multiple levels and difficulties for any kind of play.  
The weapon combos with certain perks make some really great weapons.  While keeps every run somewhat different keeps you on your toes.  Plenty of achievements to grab for those of us who like that.  
Multiplayer takes things to a different level and increases the enjoyment.  However there 
is some issues with 4 player but I’ve seen it improve.  Keep up the work devs I know your listening and working hard to make this a great game.

developer_response.body: This one gave us warm fuzzies - thank you, TbcVRgaming! Hearing that we hit that sweet spot between grind and fun really means a lot. We're working on smoothing out those 4-player hiccups, and your encouragement definitely fuels us! ❤️🍬

reviews.review_description: What a blast! Of all the VR games I've played, I'd put this second only to Beat Saber in my library. Very easy to pick up, good progression, fun gameplay, and even a multiplayer option. It's a roguelite where you fend off swarms of pinatas with a variety of weapons, the art style is fun and makes the game feel very arcady and lighthearted. I'll absolutely be recommending it to all my friends =) Still has some buggys, but the devs show great dedication to the game and players so I have no doubt that future updates will continue to make this into one of the best games on the store

developer_response.body:
That’s seriously high praise - thank you SO SO much, preso.millswords! ❤️❤️❤️

Being second to Beat Saber in your library is a huge honor. We’re definitely hard at work squashing bugs and pushing updates, and feedback like yours helps steer the future game experience! ✨🍬

reviews.review_description:
"Rogue Piñatas: VRmageddon is a piñata parasite that will since its brightly coloured hooves into your subconscious and won’t let go!"
Full review over at The Elite Institute

developer_response.body: The_Elite_Institute, thank you so much for your article coverage on our game! 🎉 Your review shines a wonderfully bright light on Rogue Piñatas and we appreciate your wishes on the success of our game. ✨❤️🍬 Thanks for all your support!

reviews.review_title: Fun experience with potential for more
reviews.review_description:
Edit: looks like my game updated and now five characters are unlocked with the last now only needing 300 kills. Great developers like this listen to feedback and make the game better. 

Rogue piñatas is an interesting game that pits you against a horse of living piñatas. 
Billed as a rogue lite, this feels a lot like a wave based game, as you're consistently facing off against dozens of different types of enemies. 
The game is fun in bursts, but can get frustrating in moments where you feel overwhelmed and under powered. 
There are 6 characters with their own weapons, and you're able to unlock more throughout each playthrough by leveling up. You do this by collecting candy from fallen enemies. 
Most weapons are fun, however, there are some which feel cumbersome. Ranged weapons also don't have a sight, so it takes some practice to shoot accurately. 
You unlock characters by using their weapons in game and then taking down 1000 piñatas. This is a very arduous process since you don't always get to select the same weapons each playthrough. Also, you only get the starting weapon associated with the character you pick, and it takes a while to unlock more. 
The game is very grind heavy, which may be a turn off for some people, but for those who want to play and unlock everything, they'll enjoy this. 
You can also earn permanent buffs for your character which help a lot. 
Overall good start and there's opportunity for more.

reviews.review_title: Perfect for the end of a hard days work
reviews.review_description: This is one of those games that's great to blow off some steam with at the end of a hard days work.  Just smashing stuff is such a great stress relief.  There's all kinds of fun different pinatas to hit too.  It's got a bit of depth to it all as well, being able to upgrade my character and to try and achieve all of the challenges.  My favorite thing to do is to launch the pinatas up into the air with my baseball bat then shoot them in the sky like clay pigeon shooting.  And its got Co Op!

developer_response.body:
Smashing stress away, one piñata at a time! We love that you turned it into your own shooting gallery mid-air - that’s some A-tier creativity, Justifun. 

Thanks for diving into all the upgrades and challenges, and for bringing the bat-and-blast energy! 🏏🎯

reviews.review_description: Uncertain what the revenge of piñata smashing might look like?  Look no further!  With a bunch of attainable achievements and range of weapons and upgrades, this heart starter is a great start to the day.  Each new level includes the choice of something new to add or upgrade.  But beware....piñata's have unlikely friends in this game as well.  This game does not require a massive space to play in, and the passthrough really keeps you and objects safe.  As long as you can freely swing your arms, this game is challenging and fun!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for such an awesome review, intjmega!

We’re so glad you’re enjoying the chaos and safety-conscious smashing—piñata revenge is REAL, and it swings back! (Trust us...we know lol) 🍭⚔️

reviews.review_description: A funny pinatas whacking /shooting with rouge lite element reminding me a little of vampire survivors. Not exactly but just a tiny bit resemblance. However, i feel like some weapons are not balanced. In a long run, melee weapons seem more powerful since it can hit multiple enemies and keep enemies away while guns has no other benefit other than range. Though extra bullets per weapon level is nice, you need to deplete all ammo to reload with a cool down. It will be better if we have an option to reload at anytime. Lastly, a little feed back, it would be nice if each character has unique skill/ability specially while using their own weapons to make each character feels unique to play.

developer_response.body:
We really appreciate the thoughtful feedback, ZnightS! 

You're absolutely right about weapon balancing - it's something we’re actively tuning. ⚖️ And great point on character abilities - we’re exploring ways to make each hero feel even more distinct. Stay tuned! 👀

reviews.review_description:
This is a great family-friendly take on the zombie horde genre.

Sure Arizona Sunshine has better game play and graphics but that's not a game to let your ten year old play. Plus, this has co-op so my son and i can play it together which is where the real fun comes in.

The levels aren't overly difficult though for us, it took a few tries to get our stride to move forward. The upgrades are a fun twist too and the way they are offered gives you the chance to choose your style if you want to play it safe with defense or go for maximum impact.

The candy pick-up mechanic is a fun little nod to the nature of your enemies, too!

This isn't a huge expansive game but it's got a lot of replay value when doing it multi-player and the enemies are cute rather than nightmare-inducing so perfect for us.

developer_response.body: ThePromee, we're so glad it works as a co-op experience you can share with your kid - that’s the kind of feedback that really makes our day. Thanks for noticing the little things too - every sprinkle of detail was placed with love 🍭🍬❤️

reviews.review_description:
A great blend of zombies ate my neighbors and a VR co-op wave survival game. 

Gameplay is satisfying, art style is Fortnite inspired colorful, and playing with friends is a blast. 

There’s a lot to the game too - great UI, solid progression and modifiers, plus characters and weapons to mix things up. 

And don’t let the art style fool you, this offers a solid challenge to even seasoned VR veterans. It’s fun to play solo and ever better with friends; highly recommended.

developer_response.body: Huge thanks for the love, alexplaysvideogamez! ❤️ We’re thrilled you’re enjoying the chaos with friends - piñata party mode is always the sweetest kind! 🍬🎮

reviews.review_description:
Honestly i had fun playing. Alot
Of weapons to earn and trying to survive as long as possible was a brilliant idea. Overall i think this game was definitely worth trying out

developer_response.body: Glad you’re having a blast, HawaiianSteve89! Smashing waves of candy chaos is always a good time - thanks for giving it a whirl! 🎉🪓
